topic: Gemma 4 Review 2026: Google's Best Open-Source Model Yet?

key_points: Released April 2, 2026 under Apache 2.0
Four model variants from E2B to 31B Dense
31B ranks #3 on LMArena leaderboard
Beats many models 10-20x its size on key benchmarks
Native multimodal including audio support
Gemma 4 31B scores 85.2% on MMLU Pro
89.2% on AIME 2026 math benchmark
80.0% on LiveCodeBench v6
86.4% on tau2-bench (13x improvement from Gemma 3)
Codeforces ELO jumped from 110 to 2150
Complete developer guide covering all 4 variants
Apache 2.0 with no MAU limits or royalties
Context windows: 128K (E2B/E4B), 256K (26B/31B)
Works with Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, HuggingFace, NVIDIA NIM
26B MoE uses only 3.8B active parameters
Gemma 4 vs Llama 4 vs Qwen 3.5 head-to-head comparison
Gemma 4 wins on math and coding benchmarks vs Qwen 3.5
Llama 4 holds context-length crown at 10M tokens
Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5 both Apache 2.0 vs Llama 4 custom license
Gemma 4 covers full spectrum from phone to workstation
Specs, benchmarks, and local run guide
E2B and E4B designed for phones and edge devices
26B MoE achieves 97% of 31B quality at 8x less compute
Hardware requirements: 14-20 GB VRAM for quantized models
Fine-tuning possible via LoRA with 100-500 examples

stats.fact: Gemma 4 31B scores 85.2% on MMLU Pro, ranking #3 on LMArena open model leaderboard
Gemma 4 31B achieves 89.2% on AIME 2026 math benchmark
Gemma 4 31B scores 86.4% on tau2-bench (agentic tasks), a 13x improvement over Gemma 3's 6.6%
Gemma 4 31B achieves 80.0% on LiveCodeBench v6 and Codeforces ELO of 2150 (expert competitive programmer level)
400+ million downloads across all Gemma models since launch, with 100,000+ custom fine-tuned variants created
26B MoE variant delivers 97% of 31B dense performance with only 3.8B active parameters (8x compute efficiency)
Gemma 4 is the only open model family spanning phones (E2B) to workstations (31B) under Apache 2.0 with no MAU restrictions as of May 2026
A 2026 Databricks report found 75%+ of enterprises use two or more LLM families in production
26B MoE at Q4 quantization requires 14-18 GB VRAM (fits RTX 3090/4090 or Mac 24GB); 31B Dense requires ~20 GB
Gemma 4 context windows: 128K tokens for E2B/E4B, 256K tokens for 26B MoE and 31B Dense

angles: The '31B beats 400-600B' story: how Gemma 4 achieves frontier performance at a fraction of rival model size via architectural advances
Apache 2.0 vs restricted licenses: why Gemma 4's unrestricted commercial license is a game-changer vs Llama 4's 700M MAU cap
On-device AI revolution: Gemma 4 E2B/E4B bring native multimodal (text+image+audio) to phones and edge hardware
Agentic leap: tau2-bench score of 86.4% (13x improvement from Gemma 3) makes Gemma 4 the top open model for autonomous AI agents
MoE efficiency advantage: 26B A4B achieves 97% of 31B quality at 8x less inference compute
Google's open-source momentum: 400M+ downloads across Gemma family shows sustained developer adoption
Local deployment: ollama run gemma4 one-command setup democratizes frontier-level AI for solo developers
